_meta.keyKnowledge: We are learning about the fundamental economic problem of scarcity — that resources are limited while wants are unlimited — and how this forces us to make choices involving trade-offs.

_source.w: We are learning about the fundamental economic problem of scarcity — that resources are limited while wants are unlimited — and how this forces us to make choices involving trade-offs.
y: Scarcity is the central concept in economics — it explains why every choice involves giving something up.
Understanding opportunity cost helps us make smarter decisions about how to use our time, money, and resources.
This concept applies to everything from personal budgets to government spending decisions.
eg: Discussing why a government cannot fund every project and must choose between competing priorities like hospitals, schools, and roads.
Calculating the opportunity cost of spending money on one item versus another.
Investigating how individuals face scarcity decisions every day — choosing how to spend limited time or money.
Exploring how businesses decide what to produce given limited resources.
Analysing a scenario where a community must choose between two development proposals.
Examining how Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Peoples managed scarce resources sustainably over millennia.
